key_insight: The paper learns how to choose local branching bounds adaptively so a reoptimization solver can quickly recover feasible log-truck schedules while exploring the stability–cost tradeoff.
engineering_relevance: This provides dispatchers with practical, near-real-time recovery plans after disruptions, balancing schedule fidelity and operational feasibility in forestry logistics.
method: Formulate recovery as a sequence of neighborhood-restricted MILPs indexed by deviation bound k.
Compute the minimum feasible deviation k_min exactly to anchor the search.
Use REINFORCE to learn which k values to try next from solver feedback and build a Pareto front of non-dominated plans.
evidence_signal: On weekly historical instances from a Canadian forestry partner, the method handled all disruption types, found richer Pareto fronts with fewer solver calls than grid or dichotomic baselines, and stayed within acceptable reoptimization times.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the learned policy and solver feedback, so generalization to new networks, demand regimes, or disruption mixes should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Learning to recover: Adaptive local branching with reinforcement learning for log-truck routing and scheduling under disruptions
